title: orobanche minor subsp. castellana
intro: Orobanche minor subsp. castellana is a subspecies of parasitic plant, belonging to the broomrape family (Orobanchaceae), that grows by attaching itself to the roots of other plants, particularly those in the family Lamiaceae, to derive nutrients. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, as well as possessing antimicrobial and antiviral properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Orobanche minor subsp. castellana is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and has been used in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions to leverage its medicinal properties.
